name: Adelaide
summary: Adelaide is a friendly seaside city on the Kaurna Plains, famous for festivals, parks, beaches, and giant pandas at its zoo. Your cruise ship docks at Outer Harbor, and a quick train ride whisks you to a city packed with adventures. Pack sunscreen, a hat, and curiosity!
funFacts: Adelaide Zoo is the only place in the Southern Hemisphere with giant pandas. Xing Qiu and Yi Lan arrived in 2024 and munch bamboo all day long.
Adelaide is nicknamed the City of Churches because early settlers from many religions built beautiful steeples that still poke above the rooftops today.
Locals call it the Festival City. Huge events like WOMADelaide, Fringe, and Illuminate fill the streets with lights, music, dancers, and giant puppets.
Adelaide sits on Kaurna Country, looked after by the Kaurna people for tens of thousands of years before any ship from Europe ever sailed in.
The world-famous Barossa Valley wine region is just up the road, but Adelaide grows fruit too. Beerenberg Farm lets families pick juicy strawberries.
thingsToDo: Hop off the ship and walk 150 metres to Outer Harbor station, then catch the 40-minute train to Adelaide Railway Station on North Terrace.
Meet pandas Xing Qiu and Yi Lan, plus tree kangaroos and meerkats, at Adelaide Zoo, a short stroll from the city through the Botanic Garden.
Explore the South Australian Museum for free and gawp at the huge 18-metre sperm whale skeleton and the world's biggest Aboriginal cultures gallery.
Jump on the historic Glenelg tram at Victoria Square for a 25-minute ride to Glenelg Beach for sandcastles, ice cream, and waterslides at The Beachouse.
Picnic under shady trees at Adelaide Botanic Garden, then peek inside the giant glass Bicentennial Conservatory to find rainforest plants from far away.
Wander Adelaide Central Market near Victoria Square to taste fresh cheeses, fruits, doughnuts, and treats from over 70 stalls before catching the train back.

localWord.meaning: Hello, are you good? — a friendly Kaurna greeting from the Adelaide Plains

images.alt: Adelaide's city skyline rises behind the green parklands and the River Torrens on a sunny day.
The big lit-up Adelaide Oval stadium glows at night beside the river footbridge.
Sunny Glenelg Beach with golden sand, blue water and the long jetty stretching out to sea.
A fluffy koala clings to a tree branch at Cleland Wildlife Park near Adelaide.
The pretty old glass Palm House sits among lawns in the Adelaide Botanic Garden.
